economic_notes: Fort McKay First Nation is heavily dependent on its business enterprises (Fort McKay Group of Companies Limited Partnership and Fort McKay Landing Limited Partnership) involved in oil sands service and development. Business enterprises reported revenue of $361,748,678 and net income of $52,348,988 in 2021. The First Nation is also economically dependent on continued cash flow from these investments.
notes: Business enterprises (Fort McKay Group of Companies LP and Fort McKay Landing LP) are accounted for using the modified equity basis as government business enterprises — not consolidated line by line. Business enterprise revenue of ~$362M is not included in First Nation revenue; only the equity earnings of $52.3M flow through. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($11,186,705) + Health Canada ($3,367,870) + CMHC ($191,921) + Athabasca Tribal Council ($605,316) + First Nations Development Funding ($607,627) = $17,959,439; ATC may or may not be a government transfer — included for completeness. OSR calculated as total revenue minus total government transfers. Investment in Settlement Trust of $63.5M and Investment in Business Enterprises of $141.9M are significant financial assets. Prior year had an impairment loss on investments of $3,500,000. No schedule of remuneration was included in the document provided. Annual debt service reflects principal repayments only ($1,590,871) per cash flow statement.
